title: Agent Permission Denied Details
description: Recommended details shape for `PERMISSION_DENIED` errors when the gate that fired is the calling buyer agent's commercial relationship with the seller (`scope: "agent"`) â€” distinct from generic credential-shaped permission failures (no `scope`) and from per-account/per-task authorization failures (`SCOPE_INSUFFICIENT`, `READ_ONLY_SCOPE`, `FIELD_NOT_PERMITTED`). The shape carries exactly one of `status` (the agent's commercial status â€” onboarded but currently `suspended` or permanently `blocked`) or `reason` (a registered non-status gate, e.g., `sandbox_only` for an agent provisioned for sandbox traffic only). `additionalProperties: false` plus `oneOf` on `status`/`reason` clamp the shape so adopters cannot invent ad-hoc per-agent state keys (rate cards, payment terms, credit limit, billing entity, custom reason strings) or carry both axes simultaneously â€” full disclosure of the agent's commercial state in a single probe is a per-agent oracle the clamp prevents. Future per-agent state additions belong on new dedicated codes (each with its own clamped details shape), NOT by relaxing this shape. The cross-tenant onboarding oracle clamp and its full channel-coverage requirements are normative in `error-handling.mdx` Per-Agent Authorization Gate â€” this schema description does not restate them to avoid drift; sellers MUST consult that section for the omit-on-unestablished-identity rule and the latency / headers / side-effects / observability parity requirements.

scope.description: Discriminator that this `PERMISSION_DENIED` was triggered by the per-buyer-agent commercial gate (as opposed to a generic credential-shaped failure). Registered subset of `enums/error-scope.json` â€” sellers MUST set this field exactly to `"agent"` for this shape, and MUST omit `scope` entirely when responding on the unauthenticated/unestablished-identity path required by the cross-tenant oracle clamp. If a future major version elevates `suspended` / `blocked` to dedicated error codes (e.g., `AGENT_SUSPENDED` / `AGENT_BLOCKED`), those codes will carry their own details shapes and SHOULD NOT carry an explicit `scope` field â€” the code itself is the discriminator on that path.

status.description: The agent's commercial status with the seller. `"suspended"` â€” onboarded but the relationship is temporarily paused (re-onboarding may resolve; agent MUST surface to a human). `"blocked"` â€” the relationship is permanently denied (no autonomous recovery). Sibling values to the lifecycle states tracked on existing `ACCOUNT_SUSPENDED` / `CAMPAIGN_SUSPENDED` codes, scoped to the agent-relationship axis rather than the account or campaign. MUST NOT be present together with `reason` (`oneOf` enforces exclusivity).

reason.description: Non-status gate that fired against this buyer agent. `"sandbox_only"` â€” the agent is provisioned for sandbox traffic only and the request was against a non-sandbox account. New `reason` values MUST be registered here (the enum is closed by `additionalProperties: false`) so cross-language SDKs can dispatch without parsing prose. MUST NOT be present together with `status` (`oneOf` enforces exclusivity).
